State "'* California 
Memorandum 
To 
Advice Request Participants 
Date 
From 
: 
CTICES COMMISSION 
Subject: 
Repayments by Campaign Committees 
April 25, 1983 
No. M-83-llS 
See ~attached letter to Assemblyman Ross Johnson in 
answer to'~advice meeting question (attached) on reporting 
repayments or reimbursement to candidates. 
BAM:plh 
Attachment 


Ad .......... oti_ ••

$tol_.,.,"," oi E~ 
, ......... 
322~ 
322·5901 
322 ...... 1 
322 ........... 
Assemblyman Ross Johnson 
State Capitol, Rm. 4130 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Attn: 
Penny Williams 
Dear Assemblyman Johnson: 
April 5, 1983 
Re: 
Our Advice No. A-83-092 
I am writing in response to your recent question to 
documents examiner Alice Hughes concerning whether or not 
reimbursements of expenses, or repayments of loans, by a 
campaign committee should be reported as income on a Statement 
of Economic Interests. 
As a general rule, any "payment 
received" is considered income under the Political Reform Act, 
Government Code Section 82030,11 and may have to be reported 
on a Statement of Economic Interests. 
Section 82030 provides 
some exceptions to this rule (none of which are relevant to your 
question). 
The Commission has established some addi tional 
exceptions for certain limited categories of payments from 
campaign committees when reporting those payments would not 
serve any purpose under the Act • 
.. 
In an Opinion requested by Assemblyman Willie-Brown, Jr., 
1 FPPC 67 (1975), the Commission ruled that a candidate does not 
need to report on his Statement of Economic Interests the 
reimbursement of his campaign expenses by his controlled 
committee. 
The Commission reasoned that, since the candidate 
must disclose the reimbursement on his committee's campaign, 
statement, which he must personally verify, no purpose would be 
served by additional disclosure of the reimbursements on his' 
1/ All statutory references made are to the GO~lernment 
Code unless otherwise stated. 


Assemblyman Ross Johnson 
Apr i1 5, 1983 
Page Two 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
In accordance with this 
opinion, and with Section 82030, payments to a public official 
from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows:-
1. 
Reimbursement of an official's expenses, or direct 
payments for the official's expenses,l/ by that official's 
controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
2. 
Repayment of an official's expenses, or direct payment 
for an official's expenses, by a committee which is not 
controlled by the official must be reported on the official's 
Statement of Economic Interests. 
3. 
Repayment of the principal of a loan made by a public 
official from his personal funds to that official's controlled 
committee need not be reported on the official's Statement of 
Economic Interests. 
Payment of interest on the loan by the 
con trolled commi t tee ;nus t be reported, howe~ler. 
4. 
Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made 
by a public official to a commmittee which is not controlled by 
the official must be reported on the official's Statement of 
Economic Interests. 
If you have any further questions about your Statement of 
Economic Interests, please contact Alice Hughes at 916/322-6444. 
BAM:km

~/ Of course, these expenses must be campaign related or 
have a governmental purpose. 
Elections Code Sections 
12400-12407.

sections.conclusion: Payments to a public official from a campaign committee should be disclosed as follows: (1) Reimbursement of an official's expenses by that official's controlled committee need not be disclosed on the official's Statement of Economic Interests; (2) Repayment of an official's expenses by a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported; (3) Repayment of principal of a loan made by a public official to that official's controlled committee need not be reported, but payment of interest must be reported; (4) Repayment of both principal and interest of a loan made by a public official to a committee which is not controlled by the official must be reported.
sections.question_synthetic: Must public officials report reimbursements of campaign expenses or loan repayments from campaign committees as income on their Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Reimbursements and loan repayments from a campaign committee to a public official must be reported on the Statement of Economic Interests only if the committee is not controlled by the official. For controlled committees, principal repayments on loans need not be reported, but interest payments must be reported.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Clear advice letter with distinct question and conclusion sections. Memorandum header references the advice letter regarding Advice No. A-83-092. OCR quality is good with minor formatting issues typical of 1983 document scanning.

In accordance with this

embedding.summary: FPPC advises Assemblyman Ross Johnson that reimbursements of campaign expenses and loan repayments by campaign committees to public officials are generally income under the Political Reform Act, but exceptions apply depending on whether the committee is controlled by the official.
